ety: sūkh˚ = Prk. सुक्ख(इ) or सुक्खे(इ), fr. S. शुष्क
def: v.n. To dry, dry up; to evaporate; to be parched; to wither, to pine away, languish, fall away, to become emaciated; to shrivel, to dwindle; — to be kept waiting, be kept out in the cold
more: v. n. S. शोषण Pālī, soso . 1. To dry; dry up ( khushk h .). 1. Sūkhe Sāvan, rakhe Bhādoṅ . Prov. A dry July, a wet August. 2. Jitne bāṛī rang raho, machak machak tolo, Bāṛī sūkhī rang ghaṭo pīyā bhaṭak bhaṭak bolo , Rus. Wom. song. While yet the field is green its bloom the lover keen enjoys, The field dried up, its color fled, dry words he now employs. 2. To shrivel; wither up. Gahrī lālī dekh-kar phūl gumān bhaë, Kete bāg jahān meṅ lag lag sūkh gaë! The flower her deep red vaunting sees and swells with lofty pride, How many gardens in this world have withered thee beside! 3. To become emaciated; to be in a decline; to pine away. 1. Āi rain chakvā sūkhā tīr bin, sarvar sūkhā nīr bin, gorī sūkhī sab hī singār rūkhā hai . Song. Jhūlnā . The duck without her love all night doth pine, the tank of water drained is dry, the fair one thin become no ornaments adorn. 2. Barse daryā sūkhe hīyā, ā…
v. n. To dry, dry up, evaporate, fall away, pine away, emaciate, shrivel, dwindle, wither. See سوکنا sūknā. ¶ sokhnā ( शोषण ) v. a. To soak up, dry up, absorb.
ex: جاؤ دھوپ میں کپڑے ڈال آؤ، کپڑا سوکھنے میں بھی تو ٹائم لگے گا
Go and put the clothes out in the sun, it’ll take time for them to dry too.

def: s.m. Speech, language, discourse, word, words; — thing, business, affair (syn. bāt)
more: n. m. Speech ( bachan ). sukhan-parvar , adj. Bigotted; prejudiced. sakhun-parvarī , n. f. An obstinate adherence to one's word or opinion. sukhan-takyā , n. m. An expletive ( takyā-kalām ). sukhan-chīṅ , n. m. A critic; hypercritic. sukhan-dāṅ, sanj, sāz, shinās, fahm, go, yā var . A scholar; orator; poet. sukhan denā , v. a. To promise; vow ( bachan hārnā , 1). sukhan ḍāl de'nā , v. n. To refuse; reject. sukhan ḍālnā , v. a. 1. To question; interrogate. 2. To ask; beg. 1. Sukhan unhoṅ par ḍārye jo haṅs haṅs rākheṅ mān . Prov. Saying. Beg but of them who smile and show you due respect. 2. Jo mushkil āsān karo to mukh se bachan nikālūṅ. Nahīṅ to chupke raheṅ, āp se phir kuchh sukhan na ḍāleṅ . Sarvar Nir. sukhan-ras , adj. Intelligent; penetrating. sukhan-sāzī , n. f. Fabrication; invention. sukhan kā pūrā . Faithful to one's word. sukhan-goī , n. f. The art of poetry. Sukhan-goī mushkil…

more: n. f. S. शूल 1. A stake (for impaling criminals); a gibbet; gallows. Chaṛhā Mansūr sūlī par pukārā ishqbāzon ko, 'Yeh uske bām kā zīnah hai, āë jiskā jī chāhe!' Mansūr impaled himself, but first all lovers counselled he, 'Behold the steps the top to reach, come on who will with me!' 2. Risk of life; danger. 3. Hard labour. 1. Sūlī par kī rotī khātī hai . Prov. The bread I eat is accompanied by the tortures of impalement. (She suffers acutely at the hands of her supporter). 2. Āṭh pahar sūlī hai . Wom. Every hour of the day and night I sufter the tortures of impalement. sūlī pe jān honā , v. n. 1. To be in constant hazard of one's life. 2. To suffer continued torture. sūlī pe chaṛhānā, sūlī chaṛhānā yā denā , v. a. 1. To impale (a criminal). 2. ( Wom .) To give pain ( satānā , 2.). Khāvand kī bad-mizājī ne mujhe sūlī par chaṛhā rakkhā hai . Wom. sūlī dene-vālā , n. m. 1. One who impales. …
1. s. f. ( शूला ) An empaling stake. 2. s. m. ( शूली ) A name of Mahādeva. سولي دينا sūlī denā, or سولي چڙهانا sūlī chaṛhānā, To empale.
subs: To suffer torture or agony (like that of impalement); to be in constant fear of (one's) life
or sūlī ćaṛhānā, or sūlī denā (-ko), To impale; to hang; to crucify; to torture, give pain (to)

(-se), To proceed (with); to act or behave (towards), to treat; to treat kindly, to befriend, to behave well (to); to be reconciled (to), make peace (with).

def: s.m. A miser, niggard.
more: n. m. sūmṛī , n. f. Cor. of A. شوم A miser; a niggard. 1. Mār sūm kī gor par tīn lāt! Beggars. Three kicks on the miser's grave! 2. Ques. Jorū pūchhe sūm se 'kāhe badan malīn, Ke kuchh khoyo gāṅṭh ko, ke kāu kū dīn?' Ans. 'Nā kachhu khoyo gānṭh ko, nā kāū kū dīn Deto dekho aur ko, jā se badan malīn .' The wife her miser husband asks 'what makes you, dear, so low? What have you lost, or is it alms perchance you did bestow?' 'I've nothing lost, nor have I aught in charity bestowed, But alms seen by another given my soul with grief hath bowed.' sūm-pan, sūm-panā , n. m. Niggardliness.

adj. & s.m. Void, empty, &c. ( = sūn, or sun, q.v.); hollow; deserted, desolate; ruined; unoccupied, untenanted; plain, unadorned; — a void, blank, &c. (see sun)
more: adj. S. शून्य 1. Empty; void; uninhabited; desert; desolate. 1. Sūnī sār se markhanā bail achchhā . Prov. Better a vicious ox than an empty stall. 2. Sau gulāmoṅ ghar sūnā . Prov. Although a hundred slaves there be, no master there the house appears empty. 3. Sonā lene pī gaë, sūnā kar gaë des, Sonā milā na pī phire, rūpā ho gaë kes . My husband went in search of gold, the country round in stillness mourned, He found no gold, he came not back, my hair to silvery white was turned. 4. Sūnā ghar bhiṛoṅ kā rāj . Prov. The empty house is the wasps' estate. 2. Still; silent; quiet; calm; peaceful. Ekānte, nadī tīre, athwā sūn mandare! 'Rām rām' japo, bhāī, apnī mokshī kārne! Alone by river side, or in secluded fane, Brother! 'Ram Ram' repeat and your salvation gain. 3. Unprotected; unguarded. Sūnī khetī chhoḍ-kar hānḍe hān kuhān, Oh to bind ku-bind saë, nāhīṅ jāt Kisānṛ . Rus. The field unheed…

My love went to earn gold, and left the country desolate; I could not sleep (or he found not gold) my love returned not, my hair became silver.

(-ko), To leave void or desolate, &c.; to devastate, lay waste, ruin.

more: v. a. S. श्रू 1. Caus. of sunnā , q. v. 1. Vā ghar kī mohe koī na sunāve jā ghar se jīyā āyā ho, Man kā mahram un des kā ek na milā, divānā ho! Kabīr. Of that home none can tell me whence my life did come, O fool! no intimate thou 'st known from that far home. 2. Kahtā hūṅ ek maiṅ to sunātā hai mujh ko das . Mīr. If I a word but say with ten she doth repay. 2. To speak at one. Gar jāveṅ tavāyaf meṅ to lagtī haiṅ sunāne, Kyā āë ho, hazrat, hameṅ Qurān paṛhāne? Old age. Nazir. If I among the Cyprians go of chaff no end I hear, One says 'O seer! to teach the Al Korān have you come here?' 3. To cause one to hear; make public. Tumheṅ to sunānā manzūr thā! You purposely said it in the hearing of others.

more: adj. S. सुन्दर Pr. sundaram . 1. Pretty; comely ( khūb-sūrat ). 1. Kyā sundar laṛkā hai! 2. Chāṅdan meṅ mat sove, rī sundar! maiṅ na kahūṅ tohe kaun kahe rī? Aj kī rain ghaṭat hai chandarvā, chāṅd ke kāran tū kyoṅ gahe ri! Song. Jhanjhauṭī . My pretty one! dont in the moonlight sleep! who else should you advise? Tonight the moon will be eclipsed, why seized should be the lovelier prize? (Rāhu would leave the moon and seize you who are more fair and beautiful). 3. Tohrā le sundar balamuā, seho to gailan par-des ho! E. Wom. song. Jhūmar . My husband's handsomer than you, away in foreign parts! 2. Good; effective. Muṅh se na bolat the so khusāmad karat haiṅ, Main ne kaiso sundar jādū chalāë dīno rī! Saiyāṅ ne mo ko garvā lagāë līno rī! Wom. song. I would not speak, so he to coax me then began, Say aint I skilled in use of powerful talisman? To clasp me to his breast my husband then began.

s.m. A distiller and vendor of spirituous liquors; — a dealer in rice, a grocer
subs: s.f. A tax on the vendors of spirituous liquors.

def: s.m. A pad placed under a saddle, saddle-pad, saddle-cloth; a pack-saddle, dorser; hump (of a camel, or buffalo, &c.).
more: s. m. Saddle-cloth, or pillow under a saddle.

def: s.m. & f. A box, trunk, chest, coffer, case; a coffin.
more: n. m. Dim. sanduqcha, sanduqchī . 1. A box; coffer; chest; case. 2. Coffin; trunk. Umdoṅ ke tan ko tāṅbe ke sandūq meṅ dharā, Muflis kā tan paṛā rahā, māṭī upar paṛā, Qāim na yāṅ pe yeh, aur na sābit wo vāṅ rahā, Dono ko khāk khā gaī, āge kahūṅ maiṅ kyā! Jo khāk se banā haī wo ākhir ko khāk hai! Nazīr. The bodies of the great in copper coffins lay, The corpses of the poor not covered even with clay, Nor this as 'twas remains, nor that entire doth stay, Both dust to dust return, what more can man aught say? Of dust 'twas made, to dust it crumbles all away! sandūq'ī , adj. 1. Like a box (a tomb, scabbard). 2. ( Arch .) Oblong (a pillar).
s. f. and m. A box, a trunk, cabinet, case, chest, coffer, coffin. (Plur. صناديق).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَنْدُوق (sandūq), borrowed from Arabic صَنْدُوق (ṣandūq), borrowed from Aramaic צנדוק (ṣəndūq, “coffer, especially one storing documents, safe-guarding precious items”), borrowed from Ancient Greek σῠνθήκη (sŭnthḗkē), from σῠντῐ́θημῐ (sŭntĭ́thēmĭ, “to place or put together”), from συν- (sun-) + τίθημι (títhēmi).

def: s.m. Thin, or fine, silk brocade; a rich silk, satin.
more: s. Fine silk cloth, satin or brocade.

def: adj. Made of brocade, brocaded.
adj. (dakh.) Golden: see سونہلا.
adj. (dakh.) Golden.

ety: prob. another form of sūṅḍkā, q.v.
def: s.m. The clitoris; a protuberance, a hump.
s.f. Smelling, inhaling; smell, odour, scent.
more: (from سونگهنا q. v.) s. f. Smell.

ety: sūṅgh(nā) + ā = iʼā = iwā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Stanch (as a dog); — s.m. A stanch hound; — a person who is (supposed to be) able to discover hidden treasure or grain, or good water, by the smell of the earth; a detective; a setter of jewels.
more: n. m. S. सुघाण 1. A stanch hound 2. A person said to be able to discover hidden treasure and good water by the smell of the earth. 3. A detective.
adj. 1. Stanch (as, a dog). 2. (used substantively) A person employed in Mahārātta armies, who is said to be able to detect hidden treasure or grain by the smell of the earth.

ety: sunghā(nā) + āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं; — and āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. Scent, odour, perfume, fragrance.
s.f. Smelling; — anything to smell; snuff, &c. ( = sūṅghnī).
more: s. f. Any thing to smell to, snuff, smelling.

def: v.t. corr. of suṅghānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to smell, to make (one) smell, to get (one) to smell.
v.t. To cause to smell, to make (one) smell, to get (one) to smell.
more: (causal of سونگهنا sūnghnā ) v. a. To cause to smell.

ety: sunghā(nā) + āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं; — and āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. Scent, odour, perfume, fragrance.
v.t. = sūṅghnā, q.v.

ety: sūṅgh˚ = Prk. समग्घ(इ) or समग्घे(इ) = S. समाघ्रा(ति), rt. सम्+आ+घ्रा
def: v.t. To smell, sniff, snuff, inhale, scent, nose
more: v. a. To smell; inhale. 1. Ek nāṛ bhauṅrā sī kālī, kān nahīṅ aur pahre bālī, Nāk ṅahīṅ aur sūṅghe phūl, jetā araj tetā tūl . Riddle. Ans. Ḍhāl . Shield. A female blacker than the bee no ears yet earrings wears, No nose yet flowers smells, as broad as long in front appears. 2. Sūṅgh-ke pahannā! A jest. First smell and then put on (your shoe)!
subs: (sūṅghtā, imperf. part. of sūṅghnā), v.n. To go sniffing about; to prowl about.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀺𑀁𑀖𑀤𑀺 (siṃghadi), 𑀲𑀼𑀁𑀖𑀤𑀺 (suṃghadi), from Sanskrit *शृङ्खति (*śṛṅkhati, “to smell”), from the root *शृङ्ख् (*śṛṅkh). Compare Sanskrit शिङ्घति (śiṅghati), Punjabi ਸੁੰਘਣਾ (suṅghṇā) / سنگھنا, Gujarati સૂંઘવું (sū̃ghvũ).

def: s.f. = sūṅghnī, q.v.
s.f. Anything to be smelt or sniffed; scent; snuff; smelling-salts; snuff-box
more: n. f. 1. Snuff. 2. A snuff box.
subs: (-kī), To sniff; to snuff; to get scent (of), to nuzzle (see sungun).

def: v.t. (dialec. or colloq.) = sūṅghnā, q.v.
v.t. (dialec. & colloq.) = sūṅghnā, q.v.

ety: sūṅg = sūṅgā+rā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. A buffalo-calf (lit. 'the little humped one').

ety: sūṅg = sūṅgā+rā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः

s.f. Scent, secret information, private intelligence; spying; inspection
more: s. f. See سونگهن sūnghan. سنگن ليني sungun lenī, To take a smell, to take an inspection.

def: s.f. A female dog, a bitch.
s.f. Sound sleep; healthy slumber.
s.m. lit. 'Very blue'; the blue shrike; the emerald; the sapphire.
adj. (f. -ā), Well-conducted, well-behaved; politic; prudent.
s.m. = next, q.v.
s.f. Good conduct or behaviour, good manners, propriety; good policy; prudence; — name of the wife of Uttanāpād and mother of Dhruva.
s.m. A fruiterer.
s.m. A kind of snake.
more: s. m. A kind of snake.

def: See سننا sunnā.
more: n. m. S. शून्य 1. ( Arith .) A cypher; nought. 2. ( Gram .) A mute.

v. n. S. श्रवण, Pr. sunanam , Pālī, savanam , 1. To hear; hearken; listen. 1. Sunte sunte kān bahre ho gaë . Prov. Saying. I am deaf with all this dinning in my ears. 2. Būṛhe kalāṅvat kī kaun sune? Prov. Who would listen to an old man's song? 3. Santan kī bānī sune prem sahit jo koë, Gangā-dik sab tīrth-phal bin asnānai hoë . Who lovingly the saint's words in his breast doth constant keep, Without ablutions shall all Shrines' and Ganges' blessings reap. 4. Maiṅ ne apne kānoṅ se sunā hai . I heard it with my own ears. 2. To hear abuse; to be abused. 1. Abhī kuchh sunoge! 2. Mār gālī sunte haiṅ, gumāshte kahlāte haiṅ! Prov. Abuse and kicks he's treated to, a manager so called, parblieu! 3. Ek kaho na das suno . Prov. Nor say one word nor hear ten. 3. To hear or entertain (a case). Muqadma sunnā . 4. To attend to; regard; mind; observe. sunā an-sunā k. , v. a. To pretend not to hear. Sone …
1. ( श्रवण ) v. a. To hear, to hearken, listen or attend to; s. m. Hearing. 2. ( शून्य ) s. m. A cypher.
wety: From Sanskrit [Term?].

ety: sun˚ = Prk. सुण(इ) = S. श्टणो (ति), rt. श्रु
def: v.t. To get to hear, to come to know; to overhear; — sun-lenā, v.t. To hear, &c. ( = sunnā); to get to hear, to overhear
more: 1. ( श्रवण ) v. a. To hear. 2. ( शुन्य ) s. m. A dot, cipher.
subs: s.m. Hearer, listener; (in gram.) the second person.
wety: Urdu verb set سُنْنا (sunnā) سُنانا (sunānā) سُنْوانا (sunvānā) Inherited from Sanskrit शृणोति (śṛṇoti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ćr̥náwti, from Proto-Indo-European *ḱl̥néwti, from *ḱlew- + *-né-.

def: s.m. pl. (of ṣāněʻ, q.v.), Handicraftsmen, manufacturers, artificers, artisans, mechanics, workmen.
s.m. A blank; a vacuum; a cypher, nought ( = sun); a mute (in gram.).
wety: From Sanskrit [Term?].

def: s.m. (dialec.) = sonā, 'gold,' q.v. s.m. Gold
subs: s.f. Gold and silver; riches, wealth; bullion
adj. (f. -ī), Like gold; of a golden hue
adj. & s.m. 'Sweet or pure as gold'; pure, spotless, innocent; — a man of high birth or of great ability
s.m. A delicious morsel; dainty food; an expensive banquet
s.f. 'A golden bird'; a rich person; a fat prize
or son-mākhī, s.f. Gold-stone; marcasite; iron pyrites.
wety: From Sanskrit [Term?].

def: Judeo-Urdu spelling of سونا (sonā, “gold”)

ety: for A. سنة, v.n. fr. سن 'to form or fashion,' 'to institute,' &c.
def: s.f. Mode or manner of acting, course or rule of conduct or of life, right way (of life); statute, ordinance (of religion), rite, an obligatory religious ceremony; circumcision; a practice or saying (or the practices and sayings collectively) of Mohammad as handed down by tradition, the traditions of Mohammad; — the prayers enjoined by Mohammad
more: n. f. سن instituted. lit . a religious ceremony. 1. Any obligatory religious ceremony, as circumcision ( khatnah ). 2. The traditional law based upon the sayings and doings of Mahommad, scarcely inferior in authority to the text of the Quran in the estimation of the Sunnīs , although not recognised by the Shīās. Mard ko chār nikāh sunnat hain . 3. The prayers enjoined by Mohammad. Farz to paṛh līye, sunnateṅ bāqī haiṅ. sunnat k. , v. a. To circumcise. sunnat-i-rakat , Ceremonial observances enjoined upon the authority of tradition, distinguished as mavakkad absolute, and gair-mavakkad not absolute. be-sunnat , adj. Uncircumcised.

s. f. ( lit. a religious ceremony necessary to be performed; from سنّ To be constantly observing, to follow rule, or from Heb. To repeat, q. d. that which is constantly to be repeated, observed or performed) 1. Circumcision. سنت کرنا sunnat karnā, v. a. To circumcise. 2. The traditions of Muhammad, ordinance, institute, rite, right way (of life). ¶ sanat (Heb. q. d. the repetition of the same course of the sun, with its concomitant changes) s. f. A year.
s. f. An institute or ceremony mentioned in the traditions of Muhammad, and not of divine origin.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic سُنَّة (sunna).

def: qurbani; the sacrifice of livestock on Eid ul-Adha
wety: Ezafe compound of سُنَّت (sunnat, “Sunnah; practice; tradition”) + ِ (-i, “of”) + اِبْرَاہِیمِی (ibrāhīmī, “Abrahamic; relating to Abraham”), literally “the tradition of Abraham”.

def: adj. & s.m. Lawful; — an orthodox Moḥammadan, one who receives the sunnat or traditionary portion of Moḥammadan law; one who reveres equally the four successors of Moḥammad (hence, also called ćahār-yārī. The Turks are Sunnīs; the Persians are Shīʻas, q.v.).
more: (from سنّة) 1. adj. Lawful. 2. s. m. An orthodox Muhammadan, who reveres equally the four successors of Muhammad; and, hence, called also chār-yār-ī. (The Turks are sunnīs; the Persians are shīʼaﻌs, q. v.).

def: adj. & s.m. (Braj) = sūnā, q.v. part. (f. -ī), Heard, &c.
subs: v.t. To treat as unheard that which was heard, to pretend not to hear, to turn a deaf ear to, to disregard
part. adj. & s.m. (f. sunī-sunāʼī), What one has heard or been told of; rumoured, reported; — hear-say, rumour.

def: s.f. = sūṅḍ, q.v. s.m. Trunk (of an elephant; see sūṅṛ).
s.m. = sūṅdā, and sūṅḍkā, qq.v. s.m. 1˚ A small brown insect which destroys grain (esp. gram), a weevil; — 2˚ = sūṅḍkā or sūṅṛkā, q.v.
